Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results say 16 O-methyltransferase homologous genes reported to be expressed in poplar bark and xylem tissues were selected, and OMT21 showed a 3- to 5-fold increase in PDC transgenic lines relative to WT.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: OMT21 is implicated in methylation of PCA to vanillic acid (VA).

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Discussion states VA serves as a flavoring agent and has reported anticancer, antiobesity, antidiabetic, antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by qRT-PCR of OMT genes and metabolite profiling showing VA accumulation; causal role is inferred from correlation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points match the Results: OMT21 was the only highlighted OMT strongly induced, increased 3- to 5-fold, and was implicated as a candidate methylating PCA to VA.

Applied_Promoters.reason: No promoter information for endogenous OMT21 is given; 'native endogenous promoter' is not stated.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: It is reasonable and directly inferable from the paper that this endogenous candidate enzyme could be useful for future engineering of VA formation, though phrased prospectively.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed presents OMT21 in Results, while Discussion refers to 'a COMT1 homologue' being upregulated; lack of biochemical validation is also consistent with the text.
